message.content:
<deliberation>
Summary of approach:
- Reviewed each main GDPR node against the entire policy chunk.
- Pulled in the 1-hop cross-references required by each node (Arts 6(1), 9(2), 22(1), 13(1), 14(1) as applicable).
- Applied the instruction that omissions are Non-Deviations unless a contradictory/narrowing statement is explicitly present.

Findings per node:
- REG-031 (Art20(1) portability): Policy mirrors conditions (consent/contract and automated means) and provides data in a structured, commonly used/machine-readable format; no contradictory narrowing; absence of “without hindrance” is treated as omission (Non-Deviation).
- REG-092 (Art77(1) complaints): Policy states the right to lodge a complaint. It omits Member State location options, but that is an omission (Non-Deviation).
- REG-035 (Art21(1) objection to e/f and profiling): Policy mentions objection for 6(1)(f). It does not negate objection under 6(1)(e) nor mention profiling or the “compelling legitimate grounds” rebuttal; all are omissions and therefore Non-Deviation.
- REG-017 (Art14(2) indirect collection transparency): Policy contains some of the listed rights (portability, object, complaint) and contact details but not the full set; no contradictions identified; omissions are Non-Deviation. Considered Art14(1), Arts 6(1), 9(2)(a), 22(1) for context.
- REG-013 (Art13(2) direct collection transparency): Similar to Art14(2) evaluation; partial coverage without contradictions; omissions are Non-Deviation. Considered Art13(1), Arts 6(1), 9(2)(a), 22(1).

Conclusion: No explicit contradictions or restrictive/narrowing statements were identified; therefore, no deviations are recorded.
</deliberation>
